title: Introduction to CSIAPPS
text: Suppose following shiny web application:
code: library(shiny)  df = faithful[, 2]  ui <- fluidPage(      titlePanel("Old Faithful Geyser Data"),      sidebarLayout(         sidebarPanel(             sliderInput("bins",                         "Number of bins:",                         min = 1,                         max = 50,                         value = 30)         ),          mainPanel(            plotOutput("distPlot")         )     ) )  server <- function(input, output) {      output$distPlot <- renderPlot({         bins <- seq(min(df), max(df), length.out = input$bins + 1)          hist(df, breaks = bins, col = 'darkgray', border = 'white',              xlab = 'Waiting time to next eruption (in mins)',              main = 'Histogram of waiting times')     }) }  shinyApp(ui = ui, server = server)

title: Introduction to CSIAPPS
text: migrate app within CSIAPPS ecosystem, can leverage several functions provided csiapps. specify institute internal API calls made (authentication redirects) application using set_institute(). run check_secrets() ensure environment variables required set. verbose argument, FALSE default, can set TRUE print values environment variables checked. check_secrets() throw error required environment variables present, making useful debugging. application, code defined outside used within server function, use global_wrapper() ensure accessible internal csiapps functions. ui, can simply wrapped within ui_wrapper(), includes additional code redirect app CSIAPPS user authentication aesthetic formatting. principle applies server function, can wrapped within server_wrapper(). Thefore, full code app, migration, look like :
code: # institute can be set to one of "csiontario" or "csipacific" csiapps::set_institute("csiontario") csiapps::check_secrets(verbose = FALSE) csiapps::global_wrapper({   df = faithful[, 2] }) # original code  ui <- ...  server <- ...  shinyApp(ui = ui, server = server)  # wrapped code  shinyApp(ui = csiapps::ui_wrapper(ui), server = csiapps::server_wrapper(server)) library(shiny) library(csiapps)  # CSIAPPS Setup set_institute("csiontario") check_secrets()  global_wrapper({   df = faithful[, 2] })  # Define UI for application that draws a histogram ui <- fluidPage(      titlePanel("Old Faithful Geyser Data"),      sidebarLayout(         sidebarPanel(             sliderInput("bins",                         "Number of bins:",                         min = 1,                         max = 50,                         value = 30)         ),          mainPanel(            plotOutput("distPlot")         )     ) )  # Define server logic required to draw a histogram server <- function(input, output) {      output$distPlot <- renderPlot({         bins <- seq(min(df), max(df), length.out = input$bins + 1)          # draw the histogram with the specified number of bins         hist(df, breaks = bins, col = 'darkgray', border = 'white',              xlab = 'Waiting time to next eruption (in mins)',              main = 'Histogram of waiting times')     }) }  # Run the application  shinyApp(ui = ui_wrapper(ui), server = server_wrapper(server))

what: Interfacing with the CSIAPPS REST API
title: Introduction to CSIAPPS
text: csiapps includes several functions make API calls CSIAPPS data warehouse abstracting away underlying HTTP requests. first generate warehouse client object using new_warehouse_client().

previous_headings: Interfacing with the CSIAPPS REST API
title: Introduction to CSIAPPS
text: ingest raw data warehouse, ingest_raw(), convenience wrapper /api/warehouse/ingestion/primary/ endpoint, can used. requires SOURCE_UUID target table, list records ingested, subject_field record can uniquely identified. records must comply schema defined target table.
code: wc$ingest_raw(   source_uuid = Sys.getenv("SOURCE_UUID"),   records = list(     list(id = "xxxx", ...),     list(id = "yyyy", ...),     ...   ),   subject_field = "id" )

previous_headings: Interfacing with the CSIAPPS REST API
title: Introduction to CSIAPPS
text: retrieve data warehouse, list_records(), convenience wrapper /api/warehouse/data-records/ endpoint, can used. requires SOURCE_UUID requested table also includes several optional parameters filtering pagination. retrieving records, flatten_record() can used flatten nested records tabular format easier analysis. retains original data payload also includes additional metadata fields unique id, timestamp record collected, relevant information. use native HTTP requests customized interactions API, please refer Swagger documentation, can found https://apps.csiontario.ca/api/swagger/. includes details available endpoints, request parameters, response formats.
code: records <- wc$list_records(   source_uuid = "SOURCE_UUID",    collected_after = Sys.Date() - 30,   collected_before = Sys.Date(),   extra_params = list(role = "primary")   ) flat_records <- lapply(records, flatten_record)

title: Helper functions for CSIAPPS
text: can install development version csiapps GitHub :

title: Helper functions for CSIAPPS
text: following environment variables must set use package: CSIAPPS_CLIENT_ID: client ID application registered CSIAPPS. CSIAPPS_CLIENT_SECRET: client secret application registered CSIAPPS. CSIAPPS_REDIRECT_URL: URL application redirect authentication. CSIAPPS_SCOPE: (optional) scope authentication request. Please refer vignette("csiapps") information use library.

code: WarehouseAPIConfig(   base_url = SITE_URL(),   ingest_path = "/api/warehouse/ingestion/primary/",   datasource_detail_pattern = "/api/warehouse/data-sources/%s/",   records_list_path = "/api/warehouse/data-records/",   timeout_s = 20L )
